message:
refactor(storage): extract collect_new_messages_for_existing_conversation helper

Two existing-conversation merge paths (insert_messages_into_existing
and the secondary upsert branch in upsert_conversation_profiled) had
~25 lines of duplicated logic for walking conv.messages, deduping
against the existing-by-idx and existing-by-replay-fingerprint sets,
counting collisions, totalling new_chars, and recording the survivors
into the pending-merge maps. Extract that into a shared helper so the
two callers cannot drift in fingerprint semantics or collision-counting
logic.

- Add ExistingConversationNewMessages struct holding the four pieces
  the helper produces: the new-message slice, the running new_chars
  total, the idx_collision count, and the first_collision_idx. Borrow
  the messages with the same lifetime as the input conv so callers can
  zip them with the inserted rowids returned by
  franken_batch_insert_new_messages without an extra clone.
- Add collect_new_messages_for_existing_conversation which takes the
  existing fingerprint maps by &mut, mutates them in place to absorb
  the new messages (matching what the original inline code did before
  insertion, so subsequent messages within the same conv still dedup
  correctly against earlier ones in the same batch), and returns the
  struct.
- Both call sites now consume the struct via destructuring and feed
  new_messages straight into franken_batch_insert_new_messages.
- Pass the previously-inlined replay_skip_log static string through
  as a parameter so the two existing tracing call sites keep their
  own message verbatim and the helper itself doesn't need to know
  which caller it's running in.

No behavior change. Locally confirmed by reading both call sites and
the helper through against the prior inline blocks: dedup ordering
preserved, collision-count semantics preserved (saturating_add +
get_or_insert keep first idx), new_chars total preserved.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
